description: Identifies avoidable AWS Transit Gateway data processing and attachment charges where VPC Peering or topology simplification would reduce cost without breaking connectivity.

cost.note: Prefer cost_report.json pricing_note over these static prices.

severity_rules.name: tgw_data_processing_replaceable_by_peering
severity_rules.condition: TGW data processing charges exist AND same-region VPC-to-VPC traffic AND VPC Peering would eliminate or reduce the charge
severity_rules.description: TGW charges for data processing on all traffic. VPC Peering in the same AZ is free; cross-AZ has transfer charges. For same-region point-to-point VPC connectivity without transitive routing needs, migrate traffic to VPC Peering through a staged cutover rather than deleting TGW immediately.

severity_rules.name: tgw_over_engineered_for_simple_topology
severity_rules.condition: TGW attachment count <= max_attachments_for_peering_replacement AND no transitive routing OR multi-account OR inspection requirement evidenced
severity_rules.description: A TGW with only 2-3 attachments for simple VPC-to-VPC connectivity may be over-engineered. VPC Peering is simpler and cheaper unless transitive routing, centralized inspection, Direct Connect, or multi-account requirements are present.

severity_rules.name: idle_tgw_attachment
severity_rules.condition: TGW attachment monthly bytes < idle_attachment_bytes_per_month_gb GB
severity_rules.description: A TGW attachment with no or near-zero traffic still incurs $0.05/hr x 720 hr = $36/month. Verify routing tables, then detach if unused.

severity_rules.name: cross_az_tgw_routing_overhead
severity_rules.condition: traffic routed cross-AZ through TGW when same-AZ path exists
severity_rules.description: Cross-AZ TGW routing adds $0.01/GB data transfer on top of TGW data processing. AZ-local routing (or AZ-local peering) eliminates the transfer charge.

safety_checks_required: Confirm topology is point-to-point, not hub-spoke or transitive.
Confirm no centralized inspection appliance uses TGW routing.
Confirm no Direct Connect or VPN attachment on the TGW.
Confirm no multi-account routing relies on the TGW.
Confirm security groups and NACLs permit traffic over the peering path.
Confirm VPC CIDR ranges do not overlap (peering requirement).
Preserve existing VPC peering resources unless explicitly flagged.
Cut over routes and monitor before detaching TGW attachments.
savings_calculation_priority: Use cost_report.json pricing_note scenario-specific TGW data processing savings.
Use metrics/business_metrics monthly data_processed_gb multiplied by TGW data processing price.
Use attachment-hour savings only after route and dependency checks confirm attachment removal is safe.
